incorrects.description: Student selected Scale of 5 — numbers are not multiples of 5

params.description: All four values highlight in data table.

steps.text: Look at the ones digits: 7, 4, 1, 3. None end in 0 or 5. That tells you these aren't multiples of 5. Which scale shows any whole number exactly?

incorrects.description: Student selected Scale of 10 — numbers are not multiples of 5

params.description: All four values highlight in data table.

steps.text: Look at the ones digits: 7, 4, 1, 3. None end in 0 or 5. That tells you these aren't multiples of 5. Which scale shows any whole number exactly?

incorrects.description: Student selected Scale of 1 — would need many tick marks

params.description: Largest value 41 highlights in data table.

steps.text: The biggest number is 41. A scale of 1 would need 41 tick marks on the axis. Which scale shows exact values with fewer marks?

incorrects.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

params.description: All values highlight in data table.

steps.text: Let me show you how to think about this. First I check the ones digits: 7, 4, 1, 3. None are 0 or 5, so these aren't multiples of 5. That rules out scales of 5 and 10 for exact placement. Next I check the biggest number: 41. Scale of 1 would need 41 tick marks — too many. Scale of 2 can show any whole number exactly because half of 2 is 1, and 41 only needs 21 tick marks. The answer is Scale of 2.
